About the job
Location: Remote

Reports to: Chief Commercial Officer

Travel: 30–40%

About SteadyIQ

SteadyIQ is transforming how eligibility verification works for public benefits programs. Building on our roots in income verification, SteadyIQ has evolved into a broader platform to support seamless, unified verification experiences across programs, data sources, and user journeys.

Our secure, user-consented approach enables agencies to access accurate, real-time information—reducing administrative burden, improving program integrity, and creating a more intuitive experience for the people they serve.

The Role

SteadyIQ is seeking a Director, Public Sector Sales to originate, advance, and close new business with state and local agencies while supporting long-term account growth.

This is a full-cycle, quota-carrying role responsible for building pipeline, leading sales engagements, navigating procurement, and closing deals. The Director will also support renewal and expansion opportunities within existing accounts. This is an individual contributor position with no immediate direct reports, focusing on high-impact direct sales and strong collaboration with internal teams.

You will serve as a primary external face of SteadyIQ—engaging state leaders, demonstrating our platform, and guiding opportunities from initial interest through contract execution and long-term growth — all well-documented through SteadyIQ’s customer relationship management (CRM) system.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and we encourage early submissions as we aim to move quickly through the hiring process.

What You’ll Own

Pipeline Development & Market Engagement

Build and manage a strong pipeline of state agency opportunities through proactive outreach, relationship-building, and market engagement
Identify and engage key decision-makers across state agencies, positioning SteadyIQ as a credible and relevant partner
Represent SteadyIQ at conferences, industry events, and meetings to generate awareness and advance opportunities
Travel regularly to meet with state leaders and move deals forward
Maintain impeccable deal hygiene by ensuring all activity, procurement milestones, and forecasting data are logged accurately and in real time through SteadyIQ’s CRM, HubSpot. 

Full-Cycle Sales Execution & Deal Ownership

Own opportunities from initial engagement through contract execution, including early engagement, proposal scoping, stakeholder alignment, and negotiation
Conduct product demonstrations that clearly communicate SteadyIQ’s value and differentiation
Translate complex product capabilities into clear, compelling narratives for public sector audiences
Drive deal progression and maintain momentum through long and complex government sales cycles
Partner with internal teams to shape and deliver solutions that meet state needs

Procurement & RFP Leadership

Lead and coordinate responses to RFPs, RFIs, and other procurement vehicles
Navigate state procurement processes, timelines, and requirements with confidence
Develop compelling written and verbal responses aligned to agency priorities
Ensure submissions are competitive, compliant, and strategically positioned to win

Account Growth, Renewal & Expansion

Support renewal and expansion efforts within existing state accounts in partnership with customer experience teams
Identify opportunities to deepen engagement and expand scope of work over time
Maintain relationships with key stakeholders to support long-term growth and retention

Go-To-Market Collaboration

Partner with marketing, product, and customer experience teams to refine messaging and positioning
Provide market feedback to inform product strategy and roadmap priorities
Collaborate with partnership and strategy roles to align on account approach and stakeholder engagement
Help improve overall sales effectiveness through insights from the field

What Success Looks Like

Strong pipeline of qualified state opportunities that convert into closed deals
Successful navigation of complex procurement processes resulting in contract awards
Expansion and renewal opportunities identified and supported within existing accounts
Increased awareness and credibility of SteadyIQ within target markets

Qualifications

7–12 years of experience in public sector sales, govtech, consulting, or related fields
Strong understanding of state government procurement processes and contracting vehicles
Experience engaging with state or local agencies, preferably in health and human services or public benefits programs (e.g. Health and Human Services, Social Services)
Ability to independently conduct product demos and communicate SaaS solutions effectively
Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to translate complex solutions into clear value
Proven ability to build relationships and close new business opportunities
Comfort operating in a fast-paced, startup environment with a high degree of autonomy
Willingness to travel frequently (30–40%)
Experience closing high-value, complex SaaS/technology deals in the public sector

Why This Role Matters

This role is central to SteadyIQ’s growth. By building relationships, shaping opportunities, and closing deals, you will directly influence our ability to expand impact and bring better solutions to the public sector.

Total Compensation Package

Base Salary: $140,000 – $175,000, commensurate with senior-level government sector expertise. 
Variable: Uncapped commission structure for new business and renewals. 
This position is eligible for equity. 
We offer a 100% company-paid benefits package for employees and their families, including medical, dental, and vision. SteadyIQ maintains a positive work environment with unlimited time off and future growth opportunities within the company. 
All work travel is fully reimbursed, within reasonable company guidelines
This compensation and benefits information is based on SteadyIQ’s good faith estimate as of the date of publication and may be modified in the future at the discretion of the company. The level of pay within the range will depend on a variety of job-related factors that may include relevant prior experience and/or education, or particular skills and experience.

Government/public sector experience — Deep public sector background with dedicated government roles at Adobe, Salesforce, and BDNA (Director, State & Local Government).
CRM/Salesforce proficiency — Salesforce listed as core capability; held Strategic Account Executive role at Salesforce directly.

rawText:
About the job
Unstructured is defining the standard for enterprise data transformation in the age of LLMs and generative AI. In just two years, we’ve raised over $65M from world-class investors, including Menlo Ventures, Bain Capital, Databricks, NVIDIA, Microsoft, and IBM.

Our open-source toolkit has been downloaded 61M+ times and is used by 90% of the Fortune 1000. We power production AI workflows across commercial and federal sectors, transforming PDFs, HTML, Word docs, images, emails, and more into AI-ready data pipelines that scale.

We’re not just building tools, we’re building the backbone of generative AI and the infrastructure that unlocks intelligence across industries and the public sector.

We are seeking a Strategic Account Executive focused on State, Local, and Education (SLED) customers to drive revenue growth and adoption of our recently FedRAMP High-certified software. In this role, you will own a defined territory and be responsible for developing and executing account strategies that expand Unstructured’s footprint across state and local government agencies, municipalities, public safety organizations, and higher education institutions.

This role requires a strong understanding of SLED procurement cycles, relationship-driven sales motions, and the ability to position modern AI infrastructure solutions to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities
Own and execute territory and account plans to achieve revenue and growth targets across SLED accounts.

Build and maintain trusted relationships with senior decision-makers, technical buyers, and influencers within state and local government and education organizations.

Identify, qualify, and progress new opportunities through complex, multi-stakeholder public sector sales cycles.

Partner closely with solutions engineering and product teams to deliver tailored solutions aligned to agency needs and compliance requirements.

Position Unstructured as a strategic, long-term partner by articulating business value, mission impact, and technical differentiation.

Manage pipeline, forecasts, and account performance metrics with discipline and accuracy.

Stay current on SLED-specific trends, funding vehicles, procurement mechanisms, and competitive dynamics.

Qualifications
5+ years of experience selling into State, Local, or Education (SLED) public sector accounts.

Proven track record of meeting or exceeding quota in complex, consultative public sector sales environments,

Strong understanding of SLED procurement processes, contract vehicles, and budget cycles.

Experience selling enterprise software, SaaS, AI/ML, data, or infrastructure platforms is strongly preferred.

Exceptional relationship-building skills with the ability to earn trust across technical and executive stakeholders.

Comfortable navigating technical sales conversations and partnering with engineering to design and scope solutions that address customer requirements

Strong strategic thinking and territory planning skills.

Excellent communication, presentation, and storytelling abilities.

Ability to operate independently while collaborating effectively in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

US citizenship required.

At Unstructured, we highly value prior uniformed or civilian public sector service.

Why You’ll Love It Here

You’ll be surrounded by smart, kind, low-ego people who genuinely enjoy building together. We invest in our team, with company offsites, best-in-tech swag, and the tools you need to do your best work, wherever you’re based.

The work itself is deeply meaningful. You’ll be at the center of some of the most exciting problems in AI today - tackling meaty, novel technical challenges that directly power real production systems used by thousands of companies, including nearly half of the Fortune 500.

We support you holistically, not just at work. From day one, medical, dental, and vision coverage (plus life and disability insurance), to unlimited PTO, flexible parental leave, 401(k) options, and competitive referral incentives, we want you focused on building, growing, and staying energized for the long haul.

Most importantly, we’re scaling fast. This is a chance to join early enough to build foundational technology, influence how we operate, and help shape the future of a company defining the next era of AI infrastructure.

If that sounds like your kind of team, we’d love to meet you.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

rawText:
Optum Insight is improving the flow of health data and information to create a more connected system. We remove friction and drive alignment between care providers and payers, and ultimately consumers. Our deep expertise in the industry and innovative technology empower us to help organizations reduce costs while improving risk management, quality and revenue growth. Ready to help us deliver results that improve lives? Join us to start Caring. Connecting. Growing together.

The Senior Account Executive (Sr. AE) is a senior, autonomous individual contributor responsible for leading and expanding some of OptumInsight's most complex and high profile State Government client accounts. This role specifically requires prior State Government experience (e.g., Medicaid, HHS, or other state health and human services agencies).

The Sr. AE is accountable for executive-level relationship ownership, long-term account strategy, growth, and retention across large-scale, multi year, highly regulated contracts. Serving as a trusted, externally facing executive partner to state agency leadership, this role drives modernization, expansion, and enterprise solution integration across Optum's State Government Solutions portfolio.

Operating in a highly matrixed environment, the Sr. AE functions as the enterprise integrator, aligning Optum-wide capabilities (Data & Analytics, Healthcare Access, Program Operations, and Enabling Capabilities) to deliver measurable outcomes, financial performance, and sustained client satisfaction while influencing without direct authority.

You'll enjoy the flexibility to work remotely * from anywhere within the U.S. as you take on some tough challenges.

Primary Responsibilities

Serve as the primary external, executive-level point of contact for assigned State Government clients, building trusted, consultative relationships with senior agency and state leadership
Own and execute multi-year account strategies aligned to state priorities, modernization initiatives, and contractual commitments
Drive profitable growth, renewals, and retention across large, long-term State Government contracts (often exceeding $100M in total value)
Act as a strategic advisor by clearly articulating the value and outcomes of Optum solutions across healthcare access, program operations, and data & analytics
Lead and influence cross-functional, matrixed teams across Optum, UnitedHealthcare, Optum Health, and Optum Rx to deliver against client commitments
Oversee account financials, including P&L accountability, forecasting, and executive-level approvals
Partner with legal, finance, and contracting teams to negotiate renewals, statements of work, and performance guarantees
Ensure delivery excellence by coordinating operational teams, addressing performance issues, and driving continuous improvement
Identify and advance expansion and upsell opportunities in partnership with sales and product teams
Represent the voice of the State Government client internally to influence product, technology, and service innovation
Maintain accurate and timely account documentation within CRM (Salesforce)
Travel up to 50% to support client engagement and relationship management

You'll be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role as well as provide development for other roles you may be interested in.

Required Qualifications

10+ years of experience working directly with State Government clients or agencies (e.g., Medicaid, HHS, or other state health and human services programs)
5+ years of demonstrated external, client-facing experience serving as a primary account lead or trusted advisor to senior government stakeholders
5+ years of experience in account management, managed services, consulting, or advisory roles supporting complex State Government contracts
Proven success building and sustaining relationships with senior state officials and executive leadership
Deep experience supporting Medicaid, HHS, or other State Government health programs
Solid ability to lead and influence highly matrixed, cross-functional teams without direct authority
Demonstrated track record of driving account growth, renewals, and measurable outcomes within regulated, politically complex environments, not just delivering contracted work
Experience managing large-scale, long-term contracts with regulatory, political, and operational complexity
Proven solid strategic thinking, analytical, and problem-solving skills
Demonstrated track record of portfolio decisions
Demonstrated executive presence with exceptional written and verbal communication skills
Willingness and ability to travel up to 50%

Preferred Qualifications

Experience working across Optum, UnitedHealthcare, or other large, State Government healthcare organizations
Demonstrated background with firms such as Deloitte, Accenture, KPMG, Cognizant (government healthcare), or similar
Proven P&L ownership or financial accountability for client portfolios
Demonstrated background in change management and large-scale program transformation
All employees working remotely will be required to adhere to UnitedHealth Group's Telecommuter Policy

Pay is based on several factors including but not limited to local labor markets, education, work experience, certifications, etc. In addition to your salary, we offer benefits such as, a comprehensive benefits package, incentive and recognition programs, equity stock purchase and 401k contribution (all benefits are subject to eligibility requirements). No matter where or when you begin a career with us, you'll find a far-reaching choice of benefits and incentives. The salary for this role will range from $159,300 to $273,200 annually based on full-time employment. We comply with all minimum wage laws as applicable.

Application Deadline: This will be posted for a minimum of 2 business days or until a sufficient candidate pool has been collected. Job posting may come down early due to volume of applicants.

At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes - an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.

Sumsub

Now we are looking for a Product Sales Lead to drive the global expansion of our Fraud Prevention products.

Sumsub is a leading full-cycle verification platform that enables scalable compliance. From identity and business verification to ongoing monitoring, our platform adapts to different risk appetites and market demands, ensuring global compliance. It allows customizing analytics and workflows with a no-code interface.

Over 4,000 clients — including Bitpanda, Wirex, Avis, Bybit, Vodafone, Duolingo, Kaizen Gaming, and TransferGo — trust Sumsub to accelerate growth, prevent fraud, and maintain compliance worldwide.

Now we are looking for a Product Sales Lead to drive the global expansion of our Fraud Prevention products. This role is an exciting opportunity to be a corporate entrepreneur and lead the growth of cutting-edge financial crime products on a global scale.

What You Will Be Doing:
Develop and execute a global Go-to-Market strategy our fraud prevention solutions
Lead the overall sales strategy across all major global markets, ensuring alignment with the company's global growth objectives
Supporting high-value deals and negotiations, acting as a Subject Matter Expert
Drive revenue growth through strategic planning, ensuring sales targets and business KPIs are met
Identify new market opportunities, develop regional expansion strategies, and drive sales in key industries
Conduct high-level competitive analysis and implement proactive strategies to strengthen market positioning
Set strategic direction for the new client acquisition
Work closely with marketing, product, legal, and customer success teams to ensure seamless execution of sales initiatives

About You:
5+ years of sales experience, experience in selling Fraud Prevention products is a must
Deep understanding of the modern fraud prevention and AI trends, including industry and geographic specificities and competitive landscapes
Demonstrated success in driving revenue growth, managing large enterprise deals, and expanding into new markets
Proven track record of bringing new complex products to the market
Experience in designing and executing go-to-market strategies for regional and global expansion while staying agile in a fast-changing business environment
Have excellent interpersonal skills, including the ability to work across a matrixed organisation, influencing, negotiating effectively with leadership and peers
Experience in running large-scale education programs for 100+ people
Proficiency in using sales analytics, CRM tools (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot), and data-driven decision-making
Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field; MBA preferred.

What We Offer:
Remote-first, trust-based culture. Work from the place that works best for you. No mandatory office days, no attendance trackers. In some locations, we provide offices or coworking spaces, but the choice is yours.
True flexibility. We do not fix you to a 9-to-5 schedule. You can adjust your working hours when needed, as long as your day stays productive and in sync with the team.
Extra time off. Your birthday is a holiday here. Add to that 10 personal days each year, seven sick days without paperwork, and extra time to enjoy Christmas and New Year. Time to rest is part of the deal.
Work that matters. Our mission is to build a digital world that is secure, accessible and inclusive for everyone. From fighting fraud to making online services easier and safer to use, your work will have a real impact on how people experience trust online.
Compensation. We offer fair and transparent pay, benchmarked to the market.
Truly global. We work across continents and time zones, with teammates and customers from all over the world.
Growth built in. Clear goals, open feedback and personal development plans.
Team offsites. Sometimes just Slack is not enough. That is why we meet in person a few times a year. Trips are fully covered, so you can meet, collaborate, and recharge together.
Getting you set up. We make sure you have access to the tools and hardware you need to do your work well.
